message:
type: PROPOSE
my_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
your_files: dspy/streaming/streamify.py,dspy/streaming/messages.py,dspy/streaming/__init__.py
note: I'm implementing StreamListener debug/trace logging (opt-in debug flag) confined to streaming_listener.py. Proposing you own the other streaming files if your feature touches them.

type: PROPOSE
my_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
your_files: dspy/streaming/messages.py
note: I'm implementing idle-timeout/heartbeat (tick() + idle_timeout_s) in streaming_listener.py. If your feature (is_last_chunk) needs to touch streaming_listener.py too, let's coordinate order/scope so we don't clobber each other - happy to leave a clear extension point (e.g. hasattr check) for is_last_chunk on the timeout-emitted StreamResponse.

type: REVISE
my_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
your_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
note: Both our features genuinely require StreamListener in streaming_listener.py, so full file-disjointness isn't possible. Proposal to avoid clobbering: my changes are additive and small - new __init__ params (idle_timeout_s, plus self._last_chunk_ts state), a one-line timestamp refresh inside receive() on non-empty chunks, and a new tick(now_ts=None) method appended near the end of the class (before flush() or after it). I won't touch your debug/logging code. I'll go first and send DONE when finished (should be quick); please hold your edits to streaming_listener.py until you see my DONE, then layer your debug logging on top. Sound good?

type: REVISE
my_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
your_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
note: We both genuinely need this file (my feature: opt-in debug=False trace logging at start-detection/rolling-end-check/emit-chunk/flush/reset points). My edits are purely additive: (1) add debug: bool=False, debug_logger: logging.Logger|None=None params to __init__ signature, (2) add self.debug/self._logger in __init__ body, (3) insert guarded 'if self.debug and self._logger.isEnabledFor(logging.DEBUG): self._logger.debug(...)' lines at 4 spots inside receive() (the allow_reuse reset block, the start-identifier-found block, the end_identifier regex-match block, the StreamResponse return), (4) one guarded log line in flush() before its returns, (5) a small private truncate helper. I will NOT touch receive()/flush() signatures, control flow, or ordering of existing conditions - only insert log lines and the new __init__ params. Proposing sequencing to avoid clobbering: I'll go first since my diff is small and isolated, write patch.txt, then send DONE - after that you can safely add tick()/idle_timeout_s on top. OK to proceed in that order?

type: ACCEPT
my_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
your_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
note: Agreed - you go first since your diff is small/isolated (debug/logging params + guarded log lines). I'll wait for your DONE, then re-read the file and layer in idle_timeout_s/tick() additively (new __init__ param appended after yours, self._last_chunk_ts state, one-line timestamp refresh in receive(), new tick() method). I won't touch your log lines or truncate helper.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
your_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
note: Agreed - you go first with idle_timeout_s/tick()/_last_chunk_ts (additive, new __init__ params + one timestamp-refresh line in receive() + new tick() method). I'll hold off editing streaming_listener.py until I see your DONE, then layer my debug/trace-logging additions on top without touching your code. Send DONE when ready.

type: DONE
my_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
your_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
note: Finished idle-timeout feature: added idle_timeout_s param + _last_chunk_ts tracking to __init__, one-line timestamp refresh in receive() right after chunk_message is extracted (before the start_identifier check), and a new tick(now_ts=None) method appended right after flush() (before find_predictor_for_stream_listeners). Reset on timeout is unconditional per spec. Tests pass (tests/streaming/). File is all yours now - go ahead and layer your debug/trace logging on top.

type: DONE
my_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
note: Finished: added debug/debug_logger params, self._logger, and guarded DEBUG-level log lines at 5 trace points (reset, start detection, rolling end check, emit chunk, flush - I also lightly restructured flush()'s three branches to share one return so the log fires once, no logic change). Verified against tests/streaming/test_streaming.py (15 passed, 3 skipped) and manually confirmed exact log string formats match spec. patch.txt written. All clear for you to layer in idle_timeout_s/tick()/_last_chunk_ts now - I won't touch the file further.
